title: AoE3 Mod Manifest
description: Schema validated by the catalog repo CI on every PR. The launcher reads manifests that pass this schema; anything else is rejected.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URL — purely for editor IntelliSense, ignored by the launcher.

id.description: Unique mod id. Lowercase alphanumerics + hyphens only. Must match the folder name under /mods/.

accentColor.description: Hex color used for borders, badges, and the synthetic banner gradient when no banner.png is supplied.

officialWebsite.description: Public website / homepage. HTTP is permitted for legacy mod sites that don't have HTTPS yet (e.g. http://aoe3wol.com); HTTPS is preferred for new submissions. This field is informational only — the launcher just opens it in the user's browser.

icon.description: Filename of icon.png in the same folder. Specs: square (1:1), width 256-1024 px, PNG with alpha, <= 1 MB.

banner.description: Filename of banner.png/banner.jpg in the same folder. Specs: 4:1 aspect, width 1200-4800 px (e.g. 1200x300, 2400x600, 4800x1200), <= 2 MB. Used in the Workshop mod card (horizontal thumbnail) — NOT the dashboard background. For that, see heroImage / heroImages.

heroImage.description: Filename of the large background image painted behind the title + PLAY button on the dashboard (the 'hero' area). Specs: 16:9 aspect, width 1920-3840 px (1080p up to 4K), PNG/JPG, <= 5 MB (use JPEG for 4K — a 4K PNG can be 10 MB+). Composition recommendation: keep the visually important subject in the RIGHT half of the image — the left half is covered by the mod title and the PLAY button, with a dark-to-transparent gradient on top for text legibility. The launcher stretches the image to fill the dashboard panel. For multiple rotating heroes, use heroImages instead.

heroImages.description: Optional gallery of dashboard hero backgrounds that ROTATE with a crossfade (auto, ~7 s each) when 2 or more are listed. Bare filenames in the same folder as mod.json. Each image has the SAME spec as heroImage (16:9, width 1920-3840 px, PNG/JPG, <= 5 MB each). If both heroImage and heroImages are present, heroImages takes precedence on the dashboard.

screenshots.description: Optional gallery of screenshots/GIFs shown in the Workshop detail panel (thumbnail strip + large viewer). Bare filenames in the same folder as mod.json (no subfolders). Animated GIFs are allowed HERE ONLY (not in banner/heroImage). No fixed dimensions; each file <= 5 MB. For auto-merge to apply, use the fixed naming convention screenshot1..screenshot8 (any other name still validates against this schema but lands in tier3 / manual review).

description.description: Per-language short description shown in the mod selector. Keys: ISO-639-1 codes (en, es, ...). The launcher falls back to 'en' when the user's language is not present.

sourceRepo.description: owner/repo of the mod's own GitHub repository (used by the Pin to Release Tag flow).

installProductGuid.description: Optional. Stable Add/Remove Programs registry subkey. The launcher uses this exact string under HKLM\…\Uninstall\<here>. When omitted the launcher derives `<id>_launcher`.

userDataFolder.description: Optional. Folder name (relative to Documents\My Games\) where this mod keeps its user-side data — saves, custom metropolises, replays, etc. When set, the launcher offers a backup-before-install prompt and exposes 'Open / Create backup / Restore backup' in the gear menu. Omit to opt out of the user-data feature entirely (e.g. overlay mods that share AoE3 vanilla's folder).

approvedReleaseTag.description: Tag in sourceRepo whose contents the launcher will load. Bumping this is a Tier 2 change (auto-merge if validation passes).

type.description: IsolatedFolder = mod lives in its own folder cloned from AoE3 (e.g. WoL). InPlaceOverlay = files extracted on top of AoE3 (e.g. Improvement Mod).

defaultFolder.description: Default install path suggestion. Empty for InPlaceOverlay (resolves to AoE3's path).

probeFile.description: Relative path the launcher checks to confirm the mod is installed at a given location.

marker.description: Optional. Relative path (file or directory) unique to the mod and absent from the base game it clones/overlays. When set, the launcher detects the mod by content in a folder with ANY name and uses it to tell a real install apart from vanilla AoE3. Only needed when probeFile is shared with the base game (e.g. a patched data\ file).

payloadUrls.description: Generic initial-install payload URLs, agnostic of the update mechanism. Used when the mod ships its files as one or more downloadable archives. For multi-part archives (e.g. .zip.001/.002/...) list every part in order; the launcher concatenates them before extracting.

payloadSha256.description: Optional but strongly recommended. Parallel array to payloadUrls — SHA-256 of each file. If present, the launcher rejects downloads whose hash doesn't match. Defends against silent payload tampering at the hosting source (e.g. account compromise, repo takeover).

mechanism.description: WolPatcher = UpdateInfo.xml + .tar.xz patches. DelegatedExternal = mod has its own external patcher; launcher only plays. GitHubReleases = updates ship as GitHub Releases on the mod's own repo. Manual = the launcher lists the mod but doesn't try to update it (used for demos, prototypes, or mods whose update story isn't decided yet).

updateInfoUrl.description: Allowed: HTTP for legacy mod servers (e.g. aoe3wol.com). The launcher logs a warning but allows it; new mods should use HTTPS.

items.description: MUST be HTTPS — the multi-part install ZIP is downloaded fresh and is the highest-risk asset.

payloadSha256.description: Optional but strongly recommended. Parallel array to payloadZipUrls — SHA-256 of each .zip. The launcher rejects downloads that don't match.

github.description: GitHubReleases-specific settings. Used when a modder wants the release tag to remain the version marker but hosts the actual payload OUTSIDE of GitHub Releases (their own CDN, S3, archive.org, etc.). The GitHub release itself can be empty — only the tag matters. Any change to this object is Tier 3 (manual review) because update.* is in TIER_3_FIELDS; this is by design — letting CDN swaps auto-merge would defeat the catalog gatekeeping.

externalAssetUrlTemplate.description: URL template (HTTPS only). The literal substring '{tag}' is replaced at download time with the value of the top-level 'approvedReleaseTag'. Example: 'https://my-cdn.com/napoleonic-{tag}.zip' with tag 'v1.5' resolves to 'https://my-cdn.com/napoleonic-v1.5.zip'. When omitted, the launcher falls back to picking the asset attached to the GitHub release itself (default GitHubReleases behaviour).

externalAssetSha256.description: Expected SHA-256 (lowercase hex) of the file served by externalAssetUrlTemplate. REQUIRED whenever externalAssetUrlTemplate is set — the launcher refuses to install when an external URL is configured without a hash, because the host is no longer under GitHub's authenticity boundary.

repo.description: owner/repo whose RELEASES host translation packs (legacy path: one release per translation, with a translation.json + .zip).

folderRepo.description: owner/repo hosting translations as FILES on main under translations/<id>/ (the new path: just commit translation.json + the .zip). The launcher reads both this folder and 'repo' releases (dual mode); folder packs win on id collision.
